note: Durable, sanitized work queue for the Blackbox Garden loop. Source of truth. backlog.md is generated from this. Never put private detail here; reference it by id under garden/state/private/.

items.title: backfill points frontmatter on existing records
items.summary: Add an optional points list (key claims relevant to each page) to the 7 existing public records so the key-points block renders and the schema-softening is real, not just supported.
items.notes: One record per item is fine if the orchestrator prefers to parallelize.

items.title: write first de-identified articles from a registered source
items.summary: Once the owner registers a local repo path or GitHub repo in archive-projects.txt, scan it and write the first de-identified build-log / entry about what it set out to do, how it evolved, how long it has been worked on, what works, what does not, and any novel idea worth a diagram.
items.notes: Blocked until archive-projects.txt has at least one entry. Intake promotes this to ready when a source appears.

items.title: de-duplicate frontmatter validation (content.ts vs content-audit.mjs)
items.summary: Two parallel implementations risk drifting: (1) frontmatter validation in src/lib/content.ts (readContentFile) vs scripts/content-audit.mjs; (2) the archive-graph builder in src/lib/graph.ts (buildArchiveGraph) vs garden/scripts/lib/garden-core.mjs (buildArchiveGraphFromRecords). Extract shared validators/builders so TS and node scripts cannot disagree. Risky because the first gates publishing; do it carefully behind agent:check.
items.notes: Strongest-tier change. Keep content:audit, build, and write-graph self-check green before and after.

items.title: expand the Blackbox Garden graph (reverse-index + series edges)
items.summary: After the base graph ships, add a reverse-index (records linking here), series/sequence edges, and tag-weighted layout so the map gets richer as the archive grows.
items.notes: Depends on src/lib/graph.ts and the /garden route existing.
